verdict: Basic companion UI may begin under canon without a fresh full-scale live claim.
ui_readiness_spec_classification: UI readiness boundary / spec-only; it authorizes first-pass companion UI behavior and explicitly defers live evidence capture, release-facing claims, and advanced rollout surfaces.
overclaim_risks: Do not claim current-live 20-account availability without fresh live truth.
Do not turn accepted development scale proof into release-ready, pilot-ready, or production-ready language.
Do not expose live evidence capture as a normal first-pass UI action.
rationale: CANON.md and MASTER_PLAN.md both say the accepted C16/C17/C20/C22/C24 evidence is sufficient for continued application development, while fresh current-live capacity claims remain separately gated. ADR-0002 explicitly says later quota exhaustion or stable-policy drift does not invalidate the accepted scale proof or block basic companion UI and application-development work. NEXT_CONTOUR_CANON_PLAN.md marks the previous repair loop as historical for development gating and names basic companion UI as the next contour. UI_READINESS_SPEC.md is consistent with that position because it defines a first-pass UI boundary over existing JSON commands and does not require new live evidence for UI implementation.
